advancedVocab.scivolare: slide;slip (here: to slide, but can also mean to slip accidentally)
advancedVocab.ascolta sempre il tuo corpo: always listen to your body (common wellness/yoga phrase, not literal listening)
advancedVocab.senti cosa ti: feel what it (truncated, but 'senti' can mean 'feel' or 'hear', here it's 'feel')

beginnerVocab.sguardo: look; gaze (as in where you look)

advancedVocab.comunque: anyway; in any case (discourse marker, can mean 'regardless')
advancedVocab.sguardo bene in linea: gaze well aligned; look straight ahead (nuanced instruction for posture)
advancedVocab.raddrizza: straighten (can be misunderstood as 'fix' or 'correct', here means 'make straight')

beginnerVocab.un pochino: a little bit; a tiny bit

advancedVocab.chiudi anche gli occhi: close your eyes too; shut your eyes as well (set phrase, often used in relaxation contexts)
advancedVocab.espiri: exhale; breathe out (specialized vocabulary, medical/relaxation context)
advancedVocab.si allungano: stretch; lengthen (reflexive verb, can be confusing in context)
advancedVocab.un pochino di più: a little bit more; a tiny bit more (diminutive, nuanced increase)

advancedVocab.tieni ancora in: hold in a bit longer; keep holding in (note: 'tieni' here is an imperative, 'ancora' means 'still' or 'more', 'in' refers to the breath)

advancedVocab.Appoggia il gluteo: rest your buttock; place your buttock (on something)
advancedVocab.richiama la gamba dietro: bring the leg back; recall the leg behind (nuanced: move the leg backward)
advancedVocab.porta avanti: bring forward; move forward (nuanced: move a body part forward)
advancedVocab.Sciogli i muscoli: loosen your muscles; relax your muscles (nuanced: release muscle tension)

advancedVocab.a pancia in su: face up; on one's back (literally: belly up)
advancedVocab.rilassiamo bene: let's relax well; fully relax (emphasis on thoroughness)

vocab.a livello di: at the level of; in terms of

beginnerVocab.arti inferiori: lower limbs; legs (note: 'arti inferiori' refers to legs)

advancedVocab.appoggio il piede a terra: I put my foot on the ground; I rest my foot on the ground (note: 'appoggiare' can mean to rest, lean, or place gently)
advancedVocab.scuoti: shake (note: from 'scuotere', to shake; can be confused with 'scrollare')
advancedVocab.arti inferiori: lower limbs (note: anatomical/medical term for legs)
advancedVocab.a livello proprio di: specifically at the level of; precisely at the level of (note: nuanced phrasing for specificity)

advancedVocab.richiama le gambe al petto: bring your legs to your chest
advancedVocab.inspira ed espirando: inhale and while exhaling; inhale and as you exhale
advancedVocab.metti una spinta: give a push; push yourself (physical movement)
advancedVocab.ti sia piaciuto: you liked it (subjunctive nuance: 'I hope you liked it')

advancedVocab.fammelo sapere: let me know (informal imperative; 'fammi' + 'lo' = 'let me know it')
advancedVocab.Ti ricordo di: I remind you to; Remember to (polite/formulaic reminder)

vocab.ci vediamo: see each other;we see each other

advancedVocab.Iscriviti: Subscribe; Sign up (imperative form, commonly used on YouTube)
advancedVocab.per non perdere: so you don't miss; in order not to miss (purpose clause, common in online content)
advancedVocab.ci vediamo: see you (literally 'we see each other', idiomatic farewell)
advancedVocab.workout: workout (English loanword, used in Italian fitness context)
